worksheetNamingConventions.description: CIQ uses a structured naming convention for worksheets and columns to manage data flow from source through overrides to final values.

.src.description: Raw imported data columns. These hold data as-uploaded or as-received from external systems.

.ovr.description: Manual override columns. When set, the override value takes precedence over the source value.

.eff.description: The effective value — resolves to override if present, otherwise falls back to source. Formula: IF(ISBLANK(.ovr), .src, .ovr)

.fin.description: The final calculated value after all transformations, lookups, and business logic are applied.

overridePattern.description: The standard override pattern creates 3-4 columns per field to enable manual corrections while preserving audit trail.
overridePattern.usage: Used for any field that business users may need to manually adjust (revenue amounts, crediting splits, territory assignments, etc.)

worksheetNumberingConvention.description: CIQ models use a numbered prefix system to organize worksheets in logical processing order.

prefixes.2Z: Key columns (special — marks which columns are override-enabled)

examples: 1B.0 Model Reference.src — build metadata, version info, model documentation
1C.0 Model Derived Formulas.src — documentation of all derived column formulas
2A.0 Crediting.eff — crediting logic with override support
2Z. Key Columns — marks which columns support override (.ovr/.eff pattern)
3A.0 Commission Calc — main commission calculation SmartGrid

modelReference.name: 1B.0 Model Reference.src
modelReference.purpose: Stores build metadata: model version, last modified date, author, build notes, and documentation links.
modelReference.usage: Every well-built CIQ model should have this worksheet for traceability.

derivedFormulas.name: 1C.0 Model Derived Formulas.src
derivedFormulas.purpose: Documents all derived column formulas in a searchable format. Each row describes one derived column: its worksheet, column name, formula, and description.
derivedFormulas.usage: Essential for model maintenance and handoff. Allows searching formulas across the entire model.

keyColumns.name: 2Z. Key Columns
keyColumns.purpose: Marks which columns in the model support the override pattern (.src/.ovr/.eff). Used to generate the override UI and track which fields are adjustable.
keyColumns.usage: Required for any model that supports manual overrides.

dataFlow.description: The standard CIQ data processing pipeline from source to payout.

stages.name: Data Import
stages.description: Source data is uploaded or synced into source (.src) worksheets via CSV upload, API, or connector.

stages.name: Data Enrichment
stages.description: Source data is enriched with lookups from attribute worksheets (territory mapping, product categories, customer segments).

stages.name: Crediting
stages.description: Transactions are credited to the correct employees based on territory, account, or deal ownership rules.

stages.name: Calculation
stages.description: Commission calculations are applied: rate tables, tier logic, attainment %, accelerators/decelerators, qualifiers, caps, floors.

stages.name: Aggregation & Payout
stages.description: Individual transaction commissions are aggregated per employee per period. Components are combined into total payout.

stages.name: Review & Approval
stages.description: Payouts go through approval workflow. Managers review, disputes are handled, final amounts are locked.

formulaCapabilities.description: Known CIQ SmartGrid formula functions and their use cases.

lookup.usage: Look up rates from rate tables, map territories, resolve product categories

conditional.usage: Conditional logic for tier selection, qualifier gates, override resolution

aggregation.usage: Aggregate transactions, calculate attainment, sum components

math.usage: Rounding payout amounts, floor/ceiling for caps

date.usage: Period calculations, proration, effective date logic

text.usage: String manipulation for key generation, display formatting

smartGridNotes: SmartGrid formulas operate row-by-row on worksheet data (similar to Excel but columnar)
Formulas can reference columns from the same worksheet or other worksheets via cross-sheet references
Derived columns are calculated — they cannot be directly edited (use override pattern instead)
Formula evaluation order follows worksheet numbering convention (1A before 2A before 3A)

componentStructure.description: CIQ plans are organized into components that represent distinct compensation elements.

patterns.description: One payout component covering all commissions (e.g., 'Commission')
patterns.when: Simple plans with one calculation method

patterns.description: Separate components for different comp elements (e.g., 'Base Commission', 'Accelerator Bonus', 'SPIF')
patterns.when: Plans with multiple calculation methods, different timing, or separate caps

patterns.description: Different components for different roles (e.g., 'AE Commission', 'SDR Bonus')
patterns.when: Single plan covering multiple roles with different structures

componentStructure.notes: Each component has its own calculation logic, can have independent caps/floors, and can be paid on different schedules.

bestPractices: Always use the override pattern (.src/.ovr/.eff) for fields that may need manual adjustment
Document all formulas in 1C.0 Model Derived Formulas.src
Use numbered worksheet prefixes to maintain processing order
Keep rate tables in dedicated attribute worksheets for easy updates
Use employee assumptions for all per-employee parameters (quotas, TSI, caps, etc.)
Build in a Model Reference worksheet for version tracking
Use Key Columns worksheet to track all overridable fields
Separate source data workbooks from calculation workbooks
Use global attributes for mappings shared across plans (territories, products)
Test with sample data before going live — use the preview/sandbox mode
